title: anthemis montana subsp. carpatica
intro: Anthemis montana subsp. carpatica, also known as Alpine Chamomile, is a subspecies of perennial flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to its high content of essential oils such as borneol, bornyl acetate, and sesquiterpene. The health benefits of Alpine Chamomile include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a restful sleep. Alpine Chamomile can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a natural remedy to promote overall well-being.
